posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 6 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others Dumont vs. SantosNorma Dumont, DECUFC might have to rig another Dumont win on the scorecards again here but it is what it is that juicy booty is getting a title shot after this whether we like it or not.Vieira vs. McConicoRodolfo Vieira, DECJesus Christ dude if this remains a striking match then were just gonna watch these two bums stare at eachother for 15 minutes. Gut feeling tells me Viera wins just because McConnico should be 0-3 in the UFC right now.Filho vs. RochaJafel Filho, SUB, R2I remember Clayton Carpenter making this guy look horrible. Easy sub for Filho here.Dumas vs. McVeyJackson McVey, SUB, R1OMG the amount of matchups on here between literal jobbers is crazy. I think McVey uses his size and BJJ to snatch up a submission here early. Dumas has ridiculously bad choke defense and struggled with previous opponents of a similar skill set.Grant vs. Luna MartinettiAdrian Luna Martinetti, DECWow an actual banger matchup on this card looks so out of place. My heart wants to pick Davey here but I actually think this new guy is going to be pretty good. Showed a lot of heart and the ability to put huge striking numbers up over the course of 3 rounds on his DWCS win. Davey being 40 and coming off a bad KO loss makes me worry a bit too much in this matchup against a young and hungry opponent entering his prime.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 6 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others Brady vs. BuckleySean Brady, DECMan... This is one of those fights that I cant feel too confident picking either side based on the matchup and how they looked in their last performance. We're gonna find out real early if Brady's glass jaw or Buckley's shoddy defense is bigger Achilles heel. I could definitely see a world where Buckley steps on the gas right away, pressures him, puts his back against the cage and finds the TKO but I think the smarter pick here is Brady by decision. We've seen him raggdoll and be dominant against stronger grapplers than Buckley and I just dont trust Buckley's fight IQ at times. If Brady gets chinned horrifically again though I could see a potential Gilbert Burns or Luque level of fall off in the near future.

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others @patrickmoore_1 He was also outgrappled by Josh Fremd, so it’s hard for me not to squint at this matchup and assume Sedriques won’t get his head stuck in the wrong place. Putting faith in Dumbass’s grappling is kinda like giving a seven year old a fork and trusting he won’t shove it into the nearest electrical outlet. Kinda playing Russian roulette at that point, except the gun is a semi-automatic.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others If this was a 3 round fight it would really favor Aljo, but I think at this point in his career he can't sustain high pace across 5 rounds, and he seems less durable than ever. He got tired in a 3 round fight against Movsar which is normal since it was full of super high paced chain-wrestling sequences, but to me it's super clear why he fought with low pace and somewhat low output in a dominant win against Ortega: he simply doesn't have 5 round cardio. I don't think Zalal gets tired easily and is a good striker at range. Good luck to Aljo trying to catch him across the octagon for 5 rounds without completely gassing out. If he can get the takedonws in early and win rounds 1-3 by establishing significant ground control time, then Zalal should be tired aswell, but I don't think it will happen. * Edited at 03.12.2026, 9:37 AM ET *

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others @voidheremking I'm sorry that someone has to say it but improving his gas tank is not plausible. He's still a top competitor and definitely top 5-10 best featherweights in the world, but his gas tank is not something he can improve as he's on a clear physical decline, which has big impact on his cardio and durability. He's still improving technically, especially in terms of striking, but he has reached his physical limit a long time ago. * Edited at 03.13.2026, 5:14 AM ET *

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: @Bilinho You're over analyzing the fact that Aljo had to fly to the other side of the planet for a fight where he didn't even complete his weight cut. I don't think Aljo was probably super locked in for that one. There was a point where he probably thought he flew halfway around the world for nothing and doubted whether the fight would even happen.Aljo took a round off Evloev and I don't remember him looking anymore tired than Evloev afterwards. That fight was just super competitive. No offense, but it feels like you're trying to manufacture a narrative here that's not supported by much evidence. I think there's honestly a better argument to be made we know 0 about Zalal's sustained cardio in a long, drawn out 5 round fight. Zalal has only seen a 3rd round once in the last +3 years and is 4-5 in his career in fights that go the distance. I could easily see Aljo taking 3 rounds from him with how good of a grappler he is. That is what Zalal has been coasting off of his most recent run but he's not just gonna coast against Aljo. Zalal has also just faced 0 adversity in a really long time and I could see him struggling here against someone who's got multiple main event wins banked at this level. Just my personal opinion, but it's hard not to lean on Aljo's elite experience here. I lean savvy vet performance by Aljo here, 3-2 or 4-1 on the scorecards.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 6 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1845 others Sterling vs. ZalalAljamain Sterling, DECOh god this going to be hard to watch for 5 rounds but I think Aljo is the much superior fighter here in pretty much every aspect. Aljo is insanely strong and his grappling dominance hasnt skipped a beat since moving up to 145lbs. I clown on him a lot for being boring but honestly this guy is one of the most dominant fighters of this era and one of the best MMA grapplers of all time. Zalal is good and very well rounded no doubt but he's got pillow hands which plays a part in this because the guys who have beaten Aljo are typically knocking him out or hurting him badly on the feet. I think Aljo mop up the octagon with Zala via wrestling dominance here but I tell you what; if Zalal wins this one Im predicting right now that he's a future champ. The Belal comparisons were a funny joke at first but the parallels between the two are becoming scary.Jackson vs. BarcelosMontel Jackson, TKO, R2IDK what on earth Jackson was doing last fight but he's such a tough matchup for Raoni that I kind of lean towards him. Barcelos is old and kind of chinny and he's won his most recent fights using his grappling but idk if he's gonna have that same success against Jackson in this one. If Jackson keeps this one on the feet he should knock out Barcelos.Dumont vs. EdwardsNorma Dumont, DECHonestly.... This is a #1 contender fight right now at 135lbs. Both women are on long win streaks at the moment and the division is lacking depth big time. I think Norma gets it done here because based off her resume she's been in there with fighters more talented than Edwards has plus she has more "pretty privilege" which I know is a thing now in the UFC considering Dern is champ.

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: Co-main is a great example of how quickly consistently finishing fights will get you noticed. Dumont is arguably the #1 contender just by virtue of her win streak. However, Allen Perez is now tied with her and at least has 1 finish to her credit. Shes also already beaten Edwards.Yet 4 finishes in a row has granted Edwards the fight Perez herself has been calling for. Just goes to show you one loss doesn't mean anything and that a fighters path can change very quickly.

posts.body: Will have to look into more tape but seeing Aljo as a (widening❗️) underdog seems baffling: Aljo has a massive experience + 5 round advantage against elite compWhilst being awkward and lacking any real power, Aljo statistically is one of the best point strikers in the sport from a significant strike differential POV.We’ve seen Zalal’s striking game fade big, both in his defeats and even in r3 vs Kattar.Zalal’s wrestling isn’t amazing. Meanwhile Aljo actively took down Evloev multiple timesAljo competitively scrambled with Evloev, won grappling rounds v Yan and choked out Cory. Zalal’s grappling has looked dangerous (even if his control has never been that sticky), but against far far lesser comp. I very much doubt he finds any sub v Aljo.Aljo’s cardio is proven, as is capacity to clutch out close rounds. Zalal probably has superior footwork and is improving more fight-on-fight but I don’t really see where else he has any edge…

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 12 Pending | Tied for 1st with 1843 others Sterling vs. ZalalYoussef Zalal, DECI feel strongly that Aljo is on the decline. Those first four rounds against Ortega were hugely uneventful—basically just pitter-patter striking where Aljo was clearly getting the better of the exchanges but by a pretty thin margin. Then, in R5, Ortega flipped a switch because his gameplan was clearly just to tire Aljo out and see if he could find a sub or a TKO late in the fight. Ortega stung Aljo with a nice overhand, and after that, Herb Dean had to get in the middle to tell Aljo that he needed to stop running away. It looked like Ortega might have been on the verge of a finish, but Aljo got a takedown and spent the remainder of the fight in top position. I thought it should have been 49-46, but I think Aljo got 50-45 from all three judges. Regardless, it must be mentioned this was a version of Brian Ortega who nearly collapsed during weigh-ins and who had been hospitalized only days before the fight took place in Shanghai. Zalal is 7 years younger than Aljo, he's got the wrestling, he's got the striking, he's got the cardio. I think he is good enough to beat this declining version of the former champ.Dumont vs. EdwardsNorma Dumont, DECUhhh I don't know. Edwards has impressed me quite a bit with her improvement over the past few fights, but her level of competition has not been the best. Her best win is Cornolle in the rematch, and I still don't think Cornolle is all that good. I mean, look what Karol Rosa did to her in the fight before the Edwards rematch. So, I'm going to pick Dumont, but I'm not loving it.García vs. HernandezAlexander Hernandez, TKO, R3That performance against Jared Gordon was pretty impressive, but apparently Gordon had been hit by a car the day before the fight or something like that, so I'm not sure how much stock to put in Rafa's win there. Plus, Alex Hernandez has been looking better since committing to the move up to 155, with that CDF knockout being being particularly impressive. I was going to pick Alex to beat MJ before the suspicious line movement led to the fight being canceled, and I think in general that Alex has better accomplishments on his resume than beating washed Vinc Pichel, washed Clay Guida, and car crash Jared Gordon.Grant vs. Luna MartinettiDavey Grant, DECOne last ride, Davey? I'm worried about how Davey is going to look coming off of a brutal knockout, plus the fact that he's facing a 17 and 1 prospect. But Luna looked so hittable on that DWCS fight, and Grant is so quick for his age. He's not going to stand around waiting to get hit like Vologdin did. And unless Davey's deteriorated a lot since the Blackshear fight, he's still got cardio and heart for days. Plus, the dude can still crack. He was on the verge of TKO'ing Blackshear in R3 of their bout. I picked Grant to beat Jourdain and we all saw how that went, so maybe I'm making the same mistake again here. Might switch, but Charles Jourdain's speed and movement is, as far as I can tell, leaps and bounds more difficult to deal with than what I've seen from Martinetti. Jackson vs. BarcelosMontel Jackson, DECI'm not loving this pick, either. Montel looked woeful in that fight with Figgy. He seemed like he was terrified of getting taken down and just couldn't pull the trigger. You would think that this loss would be enough to show him the error of his ways, but Figgy only shot like five takedowns and only got two of them. If Barcelos wants, he can shoot all day and never stop. I also really don't like Jackson's camp, which seems to have no guys who can match the caliber of training partner he needs. Still, I think that the late career run of Raoni Barcelos has to come to an end somewhere, and his wins have been just a tad sus. Talbott didn't know how to wrestle, Cody Garbrandt is washed, Christian Quinonez hurt Barcelos early and is also kind of garbage, and Ricky Simon seems like he might be fading badly based on how he looked in the subsequent Yanez fight. So, I lean Jackson either by an early KO or by a narrow decision.Buchecha vs. SpannRyan Spann, TKO, R1Somehow, Buchecha is the favorite despite 77% of Tapology picking Spann. I don't know what exactly is going on, and I also know that I hate to ever trust Ryan Spann, but come on. This Marcus Buchecha guy has just looked absolutely awful. Before fighting Buchecha, Kennedy Nzechukwu got taken down in 45 seconds by the goofiest looking shot of all time from Valter Walker, then heel hooked in an instant. Meanwhile, BJJ prodify Marcus Buchecha takes Kennedy down three times and earns a draw solely because of an illegal eye poke. I hate to pick short notice Superman Ryan Spann, but he's got a lot of power, and his losses are all to better guys than Buchecha.Vieira vs. McConicoRodolfo Vieira, SUB, R2I'm gonna be honest, I really want to pull the trigger and pick McConico. Dolfo has seemed like he's degrading quickly. Five months ago, he got bodybagged by Robert Nickal, and not too long before that, he lost to Andre Petroski of all people as a massive favorite. And Petroski has since looked like a shell of himself in the two subsequent fights since he beat Vieira—out-pointed by Edmen Shabazyan (who gets outpointed by Edmen Shahbazyan?) and KO'd with a glancing shot by goofy ahh Cam Rowston. Vieira's takedowns are pretty terrible. He's got 21% accuracy, and he shoots like a dozen takedowns every fight while only being able to get one or two. Eric McConico is nothing to write home about, but I mean, I think Susurkaev and Ruziboev would do the exact same thing to Rodolfo that they did to Eric. Eric's never been subbed in 15 pro fights, but his cardio looked pretty bad in the Brundage fight, and he kept looking to his corner with desperation in his eyes when he was on the mat as if he didn't know how to grapple. He was able to get back to his feet pretty quickly whenever he was taken down, and Brundage was taking deep, committed shots to get Eric down in the first place. Ah, whatever. I'll go Dolf. At least he has real wins in the UFC. But I might switch.Dumas vs. McVeyJackson McVey, TKO, R1Listen, this is a BADASS fight, kid. Two of the all time greats in the middleweight division. This is actually kind of a winnable fight for Dumas. McVey's grappling is nonexistent. He tapped almost as soon as he went to the mat with Brunno Ferreira, and he was piecing up Zach Reese badly until Reese got him to the mat and found the rear naked choke. So, I have very little confidence in McVey. But I just can't pick Sedriques "Boonk Gang" Dumas to win a fight in 2026. All he does is smoke weed and commit crime. And make YouTube videos. At least McVey is likely taking his career seriously.Bueno Silva vs. MontagueMichelle Montague, DECMBS is washedGriffin vs. ValenzuelaVictor Valenzuela, TKO, R1I mean, this is a DWCS loser against a vet who just went to a split decision with Chris "Action Man" Curtis, AND took a round off of Michael Morales, AND went to a DEC with Morales. The more I think about this, the less confident I am in Valenzuela, but he's 8 years younger against a 40 year old man who was never all that good to begin with.Alencar vs. PolastriJulia Polastri, DECClose fight here. Polastri plays around too much in the cage, and I can definitely see a world where Alencar gets her on the ground and wins via control time in the same way that Jasmine and Loopy did, but I think Jasmine and Loopy are better than Alencar, and I think Polastri is going to keep improving given how young she is to be at this level in the UFC. On the feet, I don't think it will be close, and Alencar seemed to be fading pretty badly by R3 of her fight with Carnelossi even though she was having the exact kind of fight she wanted. Plus, it is hard not to have any questions about those 9 grappling losses on Alencar's resume in the first half of her career. Obviously, the fact that she's competing and losing means that she's getting better, but it still makes me hesitant to pick her over a dangerous up and comer. * Edited at 04.19.2026, 12:39 PM ET *
